Article Title: Employees on Meta's internal forum after company announces thousands of layoffs coming: Living through '28 days of hell', how do you…
Article Text:
Meta employees flood internal posts with dark humor, questions, and dread

Meta is spending aggressively on AI as it tries to close the gap on OpenAI and Google





Meta confirmed on Thursday it is cutting roughly 10% of its workforce—about 8,000 employees—with the axe falling on May 20. Another 6,000 open roles will simply be closed, never filled. The announcement came via an internal memo from chief people officer Janelle Gale, who acknowledged the plan had already leaked and decided to get ahead of it. The numbers are large, the date is set, but the names aren't—and that one detail is turning the next four weeks inside the company into something employees are already calling "28 days of hell."On Meta's internal platform, employees responded to Gale's post with a mix of jokes, elephant memes (a nod to leadership finally addressing the elephant in the room), and pointed questions about stock vests and travel restrictions. On the anonymous workplace app Blind, the mood was heavier. One post, according to Business Insider, was simply titled "28 days of hell." Another asked: "How are you motivating yourself to work for the next 1 month with layoffs confirmed?" A reply cut right to it: "I'm motivating myself to do stuff that I can put on my resume for my next job lol. "One employee told Business Insider that the confirmation, despite its weight, actually brought some relief—the cuts had been so widely discussed internally that at least the uncertainty had a shape now.But not everyone felt that way. Another said the announcement added pressure to show results fast."I'm a little stressed about making impact in the next month," they told Business Insider.The layoffs are framed by leadership as a necessary trade-off. Meta is projecting capital expenditure of $115 billion to $135 billion this year—nearly double last year's $72 billion—much of it earmarked for AI infrastructure. Zuckerberg has made no secret that AI is the company's dominant priority, and roles not aligned with that direction are increasingly hard to justify.US employees being let go will receive 16 weeks of base pay plus two additional weeks per year of service, along with 18 months of COBRA health coverage.For some at Meta, it isn't even their first rodeo. One Blind user, a veteran of multiple layoff cycles at the company since 2022, wasn't exactly surprised—but wasn't exactly reassured either. "I assume I'm always two months away from being laid off, no matter what leadership says," they wrote.
Article Summary: About 8,000 employees at Meta will be laid off on May 20. Another 6,000 open roles will simply be closed, never filled. The announcement came via an internal memo from chief people officer Janelle Gale. One employee said the confirmation brought some relief.

Article Title: Paypal founder Peter Thiel buys $12 million mansion in Buenos Aires: Report
Article Text:
Peter Thiel’s Argentina property details







Paypal founder Peter Thiel has purchased a mansion in Buenos Aires for about $12 million, according to a Forbes Argentina report. The property is located on Dardo Rocha Street in Barrio Parque, an area within Palermo Chico known for large homes and embassy residences. The purchase adds to Thiel’s global real estate holdings and comes as he has been spending time in Argentina and meeting local officials. The report said Thiel has shown interest in Argentina and has met with President Javier Milei. It added that his presence in the country may be linked to broader investment considerations. Peter Thiel has also invested in properties in other countries, including New Zealand, as part of his global holdings.As per the Forbes Argentina report, Peter Thiel’s mansion spans about 1,600 square meters, or around 17,200 square feet. It was designed by Argentine architect Alejandro Bustillo. The house includes formal living and dining areas, multiple bedrooms, office space, a wine cellar and a garden-facing terrace.The property is located in a part of the city where such homes are limited and rarely available for sale. The report said the interior has been rebuilt while maintaining the original structure.The house is in Barrio Parque, described as one of the city’s high-value residential areas. It is near homes owned by public figures, including Argentine television personality Susana Giménez. The report said increased private security presence has been noticed around the property.The purchase price is reported to be among the highest for residential transactions in the area. According to a real estate firm cited in the report, such transactions are influenced by factors such as location and limited availability of similar properties.“It is very cheap for a world capital city,” Buenos Aires real estate consulting firm, BuySell BA, noted in a widely-circulated post flagging the deal. “It won’t be his last property purchase in Buenos Aires. And more of his associates and colleagues are also looking,” BuySell BA added.
Article Summary: Paypal founder Peter Thiel has purchased a mansion in Buenos Aires for about $12 million, according to a Forbes Argentina report. The property is located on Dardo Rocha Street in Barrio Parque, an area within Palermo Chico known for large homes and embassy residences.

Article Title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy to Gen Z: Don’t expect a great job straight out of college, you must ...
Article Text:
Andy Jassy, CEO of Amazon

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy: Careers may not follow a fixed path

Poll What do you think is the best approach for young professionals starting their careers? Start at the bottom and learn Aim for higher positions from the start





Amazon CEO Andy Jassy once shared an advice for young professionals entering the workforce, saying that expecting a top job right after college may not match how careers usually develop. Speaking on Capital Group’s Power of Advice podcast earlier this year, Jassy said that early career growth often starts with smaller roles and requires time, effort and learning. Jassy said that building a career requires starting from the ground level and gaining experience step by step. “If you aren’t willing to start at the bottom and pay your dues, it’s unlikely that you’re going to ever be successful,” he said. “You have to be willing to start at the bottom. You have to do whatever people ask you to do, within reason.”He added that reliability and attention to detail play an important role in career growth. According to him, employees who are consistent in their work and open to learning tend to move forward over time.Andy Jassy pointed to continuous learning as a key factor for long-term success. “You just have to be a learning machine,” he said.He noted that people who keep improving their skills and adapt to new ways of working are more likely to grow in their careers.Jassy also spoke about his own career path, which included roles outside the technology sector before joining Amazon. He said his journey involved trying different fields before finding the right fit.“It’s great to have an idea,” he said. “But it’s very useful to try a lot of different things to figure out what you don’t like and what you do like.” “You never know which things you’re going to like. In my lifetime, I have not predicted the things that I have loved,” he added.Jassy said that the current job market can make it harder for young people to plan their careers early. He noted that many feel pressure to decide their long-term path at a young age, but suggested that flexibility and willingness to learn can help.
Article Summary: Andy Jassy said that early career growth often starts with smaller roles and requires time, effort and learning. “You just have to be a learning machine,” he said. According to him, employees who are consistent in their work and open to learning tend to move forward over time.

Article Title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella may have just agreed with VP Rajesh Jha on the solution to software companies’ biggest fear
Article Text:
Microsoft Foundry : Every Agent gets its own computer



Rajesh Jha's argument: Fewer humans, but more seats







The rise of agentic AI has triggered a crises in the enterprise software industry. The fear goes something like this: if artificial intelligence (AI) makes every worker more productive, companies will need fewer employees. Fewer employees means fewer software licences, which in turn means the business model that has made enterprise software one of the most profitable sectors in technology begins to crack. Recently, Microsoft VP Rajesh Jha provided a business idea, which company CEO Satya Nadella seems to have agreed upon.This week, Microsoft offered what may be its most concrete answer yet to that fear. Microsoft announced an update to its Foundry platform, which is the infrastructure layer that allows businesses to build and deploy AI agents. The new feature, called Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service, is now available in public preview. The announcement came with a striking line by Nadella: “Every agent will need its own computer. ”Microsoft is now offering each AI agent its own dedicated, enterprise-grade sandbox in the cloud – a private, secure computing environment with its own storage, its own identity, and its own set of permissions. In plain terms, think of it as giving every AI assistant its own locked office in a high-security building, with its own keycard and its own filing cabinet.“Every agent will need its own computer. And with new Hosted agents in Foundry, every agent gets its own dedicated enterprise-grade sandbox, with durable state, built-in identity and governance, and support for any harness or framework,” Nadella said in a post on X, while announcing Microsoft Foundry update.Rajesh Jha, Microsoft's Executive Vice President of Experiences and Devices, has been making an argument that directly addresses the fear driving anxiety across the software sector. Earlier this month, he said that if AI agents are going to operate inside enterprise software systems, they will need their own logins, their own inboxes, and their own digital identities. In other words, they will function as users. And if they function as users, they may need to be licensed as users.“All of those embodied agents are seat opportunities,” Jha said, using the industry term for paid software licences.Jha explained that if company has 50 human employees, they pay for 50 employees. Now with AI agents, even if a company fires 40 human staff, and deploy 40 AI agents, this means that they still have pay for 50 total licenses – 10 humans and 40 AI agents. Consequently, a business that reduces its headcount to save money may actually end up spending more on software to “seat” the digital agents replacing them.
Article Summary: Microsoft announced an update to its Foundry platform, which is the infrastructure layer that allows businesses to build and deploy AI agents. The new feature, called Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service, is now available in public preview. The announcement came with a striking line by Nadella: “Every agent will need its own computer”

Article Title: Quote of the day by ‘Godfather of AI’ Geoffrey Hinton: "We need AI mothers rather than AI assistants. An assistant is someone you can fire. You can’t fire your mother, thankfully."
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Geoffrey Hinton

Quote of the day by Geoffrey Hinton



"We need AI mothers rather than AI assistants. An assistant is someone you can fire. You can’t fire your mother, thankfully."



Understanding the meaning behind the quote by Geoffrey Hinton



Why AI is often compared to assistants today



The concept of responsibility in AI systems



Stopping harmful actions

Giving people safer choices

Helping people make better choices

Taking into account the long-term effects

How this idea connects to current AI concerns



Moving from tools to trusted systems



Help people make better choices

Give fair information

Promote responsible behaviour

Challenges in creating responsible AI systems

Understanding what it means to be responsible in different situations

Making choices without being biased

Making sure that AI systems know exactly how they work

Finding a balance between letting users control things and steering the system

Geoffrey Hinton’s role in shaping modern AI



Why this quote by Geoffrey Hinton is relevant in today’s world



The future direction of AI development



Making AI systems less dangerous

Making it easier to choose

Getting users to trust you

Making sure of the benefits in the long run

What this quote means for everyday users



Final takeaway: A new way to think about AI







AI is becoming a part of everyday life, from apps for phones to tools for the office and smart home devices. Most people think of AI as a tool that helps people do things, answer questions, or get work done more quickly. Geoffrey Hinton, one of the first people to work on modern AI, sees it in a very different way. His quote makes it sound like AI shouldn't just be a tool that does what it's told; it should also be a "mother" figure that protects, guides, and helps. This idea might sound strange at first, but it shows that people are worried about how AI systems are built and used. Experts are talking more and more about not only what AI can do, but also how it should act as it gets stronger. Hinton's statement starts a very important conversation about trust, responsibility, and the future of AI in our lives.Geoffrey Hinton's statement makes a comparison between two very different jobs: being a mother and being an assistant. Someone who works as an assistant follows orders and can be fired or replaced. A mother, on the other hand, is seen as someone who cares for, guides, and is responsible for a long time.Hinton brings up an important point about AI development by making this comparison. Most current AI systems are made to do things when users tell them to. They are tools that do what you tell them to do without thinking about the long-term effects or moral issues.The concept of "AI mothers" implies systems that are more accountable, safeguarding, and adept at steering users towards improved choices. It shows that AI is moving from being task-based to being relationship-based.Most AI tools that are available today are designed to help people. They help with writing, making plans, finding your way around, and answering questions. These systems are meant to make work easier and more productive.This assistant model is good for a lot of things, but it also has some flaws. Because assistants do what they're told, they might not question requests that are wrong or harmful. They are meant to serve, not to lead.Hinton's quote suggests that this method might not be enough as AI gets stronger and becomes a part of everyday life.Responsibility is one of the main ideas behind Hinton's statement. A "mother" figure is someone who cares for you, protects you, and thinks about the future. When you apply this idea to AI, you make systems that care about the user's well-being instead of just getting things done.This could mean:To make sure that these kinds of systems act responsibly, they would need to be carefully programmed and designed.AI is growing quickly, and this has made people all over the world worry about things like misuse, false information, and a lack of accountability. As AI systems get better, the risks that come with them get bigger.Hinton is one of the experts who has said they are worried about what will happen with AI in the future. His quote shows that we need to make things safer and think more carefully about how we design them.Instead of just thinking about how well their code works and how quickly it runs, developers are being told to think about ethics, safety, and how it affects people.People's views on technology are changing in a bigger way than just changing "assistant" to "mother-like" AI. People might start to see AI systems as more than just tools. They might start to trust them and ask them for help.This doesn't mean getting rid of relationships between people; it means making systems that help people in a more meaningful way.For instance, AI could:These kinds of changes would mean that AI design and regulation would have to be done in new ways.The idea of "AI mothers" is interesting, but putting it into action is hard. Several problems need to be solved:These problems show how important it is for scientists, politicians, and tech companies to work together.Geoffrey Hinton is famous for his work in artificial intelligence, especially on neural networks. Deep learning and machine learning have gotten a lot better because of his work.He has also talked about how AI can be dangerous, especially as systems get better. What he said recently shows that experts are getting more and more worried about how AI will affect society in the long run.As AI tools become more common, more and more people are using them. The nature of these interactions is becoming more and more important.People think differently when they read Hinton's quote. It doesn't care what AI can do; it cares what AI should do. This difference is important because technology is always changing.The idea also shows how important it is to trust people. People are more likely to trust systems that put their health and safety first.The concept of "AI mothers" may influence future research and development in artificial intelligence. It suggests a move toward systems that are smart, responsible, and moral.Developers might want to focus on:This way of thinking fits in with the global conversation about responsible AI and technology that is good for people.People might not change how they use AI right away after Hinton's statement, but it does show how important it is to be aware. If users know how AI works and what it can't do, they can make better choices.It also stresses how important it is to use technology carefully. As AI systems get better, users will have a say in how they are used and controlled.Geoffrey Hinton's quote makes us think about AI's future in a new way. Instead of calling AI a "assistant," he calls it a "mother," which shifts the focus from functionality to responsibility.As AI gets better, this idea could change how systems are built and used. People are worried about the role of technology in society, as shown by the focus on care, guidance, and long-term thinking.This idea is still just a theory, but it points to an important direction for the future of AI. It looks at not only what technology can do, but also how it affects people's lives.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by Geoffrey Hinton: "We need AI mothers rather than AI assistants" Most people think of AI as a tool that helps people do things, answer questions, or get work done more quickly. Hinton's quote suggests that this method might not be enough as AI gets stronger and becomes a part of everyday life.

Article Title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rockets whose photos Jeff Bezos shared and Elon Musk congratulated 'failed to deliver its mission' minutes later
Article Text:
The Blue Origin New Glenn rocket lifts off from LC36 at the Cape Canaveral Space Force station, in Cape Canaveral, Fla. (AP Photo/John Raoux)

Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp on mission's 'failure'

FAA grounds Blue Origin's New Glenn





Earlier this week, Blue Origin founder Jeff Bezos shared a photo of the space company's New Glenn rockets. Bezos' post on X, formerly Twitter, got 'Congrats' message from CEO of Blue Origin's biggest rival Spacex. This was the first time Blue Origin re-used a previously flown New Glenn booster — the same one that flew during New Glenn’s second mission. Roughly 10 minutes after liftoff, the booster came back down and landed on a drone ship in the ocean, just like it had last November. The footage of the booster’s landing on Drone ship is what Bezos shared on X, and Elon Musk offered his 'congratulations' on.However, after initial lift-off and landing the rockets failed at its primary mission: Delivering a communications satellite to orbit for customer AST SpaceMobile. Roughly two hours after the launch, Blue Origin itself announced in its own post that the New Glenn upper stage placed AST SpaceMobile’s satellite in an “off-nominal orbit.” In layman's language, the rocket looked good on the way up, but the upper stage wasn’t able to put its payload into the correct orbit.AST SpaceMobile too issued a statement that the upper stage of the New Glenn rocket placed BlueBird 7 satellite into an orbit that was “lower than planned.”The satellite successfully separated from the rocket and powered on, the company said, but the altitude is too low “to sustain operations” and will now have to be de-orbited — left to burn up in the atmosphere of Earth.Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp too said in a post that the company "clearly didn't deliver the mission". He wrote, "Now that we have a more complete view, we wanted to provide an update on our NG-3 mission. While we are pleased with the nominal booster recovery, we clearly didn't deliver the mission our customer wanted, and our team expects. Early data suggest that on our second GS2 burn, one of the BE-3U engines didn’t produce sufficient thrust to reach our target orbit. Blue Origin is leading the anomaly investigation with FAA oversight to learn from the data and implement the improvements needed to quickly return to flight operations. We have been in steady communication with the team at AST SpaceMobile, we appreciate their partnership, and we’re looking forward to many flights together."This represents the first major failure for Blue Origin’s New Glenn programme, which only made its first flight in January 2025. This was reportedly the second mission where New Glenn carried a customer payload to space, after launching twin spacecraft bound for Mars on behalf of NASA last November.The Federal Aviation Administration has grounded Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket following launch from Cape Canaveral. “The FAA is aware that Blue Origin New Glenn 3 experienced a mishap during the second-stage flight sequence following a successful launch,” according to an FAA . “The FAA notified NASA, the NTSB, and the U.S. Space Force about the classification of the incident.”FAA guidelines will require Blue Origin to complete an investigation into the event before the rocket is allowed to fly again. “A mishap investigation is designed to enhance public safety, determine the root cause of the event, and identify corrective actions to avoid it from happening again. A return to flight is based on the FAA determining that any system, process, or procedure related to the mishap does not affect public safety. ”The FAA has reportedly grounded several rockets because of mishaps in recent years, including SpaceX’s Falcon 9 and in-development Starship. Blue Origin had its smaller, suborbital New Shepard rocket grounded as well.
Article Summary: FAA grounds Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket following launch from Cape Canaveral. This represents the first major failure for Blue Origin’s New Glenn programme, which only made its first flight in January 2025. This was the second mission where New Glenn carried a customer payload to space, after launching twin spacecraft bound for Mars last November.

Article Title: CoRover and Intel demo an BharatGPT AI agent that works entirely offline on Ultra Series 3 processor
Article Text:
Who actually needs offline AI





BharatGPT-mini ran on a laptop—no internet, no cloud. CoRover.ai showed off BharatGPT-mini at Intel's AI PC Innovation Event in Conrad Bengaluru on April 24. The small language model ran as a conversational AI agent on Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 processor, handling text, voice, image, and video in multiple Indian and international languages—all without an internet connection.The model processes everything on-device using Intel's OpenVINO toolkit, which optimises inference across the chip's CPU, NPU, and accelerators.Patchy connectivity is one reason to care. Defence, healthcare, and banking are another—sectors where sending data to an external server isn't always an option, legally or practically. On-device processing keeps everything local.CoRover CEO Ankush Sabharwal, speaking at the event, described a three-tier architecture: on-device for speed and privacy, on-premise for control, and cloud for scale. His point was that each layer serves a different purpose, and on-device handles the cases where the other two simply aren't available.CoRover already has deployments across government, BFSI, education, and defence verticals. The Intel demo extends that into individual PCs—field offices, classrooms, and desks where connectivity is unreliable or data sensitivity is high.The broader pitch from Sabharwal: cloud AI made the technology accessible, but on-device is what makes it usable in the places that have so far been left out.
Article Summary: CoRover.ai showed off BharatGPT-mini at Intel's AI PC Innovation Event in Bengaluru. The small language model ran as a conversational AI agent on Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 processor. It handled text, voice, image, and video in multiple Indian and international languages.

Article Title: Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth: When I start to experience the stress, that's a ...
Article Text:
Meta CTO shared coping strategies for stress

Meta CTO to engineers looking to join technology industry

Poll What is your primary method for coping with stress? Exercise Meditation Talking to friends/family





Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, who oversees the company’s Reality Labs division, says that stress is not something he encounters often, but when it surface, he treats it as a signal to reprioritise. According to a report by Business Insider, speaking during an Instagram ‘ask-me-anything’ session, Bosworth explained that he usually feels stressed only four or five times a year, and the trigger is almost always the same: being over-scheduled. Bosworth added that when he starts to feel stress, it’s mainly because he worries he won’t have enough tome for the ‘important work’. He described busyness as the root cause, noting: “When I start to experience the stress, that’s a useful signal for me. What is the important work that, if you dedicated the time to it, you would be okay? And, how do we reprioritise the urgent stuff that’s being noisy?”Bosworth also talked about some coping mechanisms which help to get out of a stressful situation. He explained that in order to manage stress he relies on conventional methods such as deep breathing, exercise, and spending time with his wife and kids. He also talks about stress opening, often with his wide, which he says helps him to reset. While he doesn’t consider his strategies unique, he emphasised that they are effective in keeping him grounded.Executives across industries use different approaches to cope with high-pressure roles.Some CEOs practice yoga, take long walks, or spend time in nature. Meta’s CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously told podcaster Joe Rogan that waking up and checking his phone felt like being “punched in the stomach,” and he manages stress by exercising in the mornings.Recently, Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth had a simple answer when a student asked how to enter the tech industry: stop waiting and start building. During an Instagram “Ask Me Anything” session this week, Bosworth was asked by a college freshman what it takes to break into technology. His response was direct and practical — focus less on theory and more on doing. “You just have to immerse yourself in it,” Bosworth said. “You should just constantly be building.”“That's what's going to give you the best chance of having the relevant skill set that is needed to make a difference in technology,” he said.
Article Summary: Andrew Bosworth, Meta’s chief technology officer, says stress is not something he encounters often, but when it surface, he treats it as a signal to reprioritise. The trigger is almost always the same: being over-scheduled. Bosworth also talks about stress opening, often with his wide, which he says helps to reset.

Article Title: John Ternus: Who is John Ternus, Tim Cook’s successor, and Apple’s new CEO
Article Text:
John Ternus Apple CEO

John Ternus’ early career and rise within Apple

What challenges await Apple's next CEO

Poll Which product innovation do you think had the biggest impact? iPad AirPods iPhone MacBook Neo





Apple has announced that John Ternus will succeed Tim Cook to become the new CEO on September 1, 2026. Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years, will now transition to the role of executive chairman for the board. Ternus, who is Apple’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, has been a central figure in shaping the company’s most iconic products and is now been tasked with steering Apple into its next era of innovation. The transition, which was approved unanimously by the Board of Directors, follows a thoughtful, long-term succession planning process. Cook will continue in his role as CEO through the summer as he works closely with Ternus on a smooth transition. As executive chairman, Cook will assist with certain aspects of the company, including engaging with policymakers around the world.John Ternus joined Apple in 2001 as part of the product design team and became a vice president of Hardware Engineering in 2013. He brought with him a strong background in mechanical engineering. He earned his Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and started his career at Virtual Research Systems, where he worked on advanced virtual reality technologies.Throughout his tenure at Apple, Ternus has overseen hardware engineering work on a variety of groundbreaking products across every category. He was instrumental in the introduction of multiple new product lines, including iPad and AirPods, as well as many generations of products across iPhone, Mac, and Apple Watch. Ternus’s work on Mac has helped the category become more powerful and more popular globally than at any time in its 40-year history. That includes the recent introduction of MacBook Neo, an all-new laptop that makes the Mac experience even more accessible to more people around the world.This past fall, his team’s efforts were on full display with the introduction of a redefined iPhone lineup, including the incredibly powerful iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max, the radically thin and durable iPhone Air, and the iPhone 17, which has been an incredible upgrade for users.With John Ternus as the new CEO, Apple gets a hardware leader at a pivotal moment. While Apple has seen tremendous success with iPhones and products like AirPods, the company has struggled in areas such as mixed reality, generative AI, the smart home and autonomous vehicles, according to Bloomberg.Apple has been spending significantly less on AI investments compared to rivals Meta, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. The company's AI-enabled Siri, originally slated for 2025, has been delayed until 2026 or later due to technical challenges. Apple has also lost several senior AI team members since January, many of whom have joined Meta's AI and Superintelligence Labs during talent wars this year.Despite these concerns, Apple is expecting strong end-of-year sales, with year-on-year revenue growth of 10% to 12% for its holiday quarter ending in December, fueled by the iPhone 17 model release. The company's shares are trading close to an all-time high following strong October earnings, though its roughly 12% stock price increase this year lags behind Big Tech rivals Alphabet, Nvidia and Microsoft.The reshuffle extends past the corner office. Johny Srouji, Apple's silicon architect, has been promoted to chief hardware officer, absorbing Ternus's old portfolio. Tom Marieb takes over day-to-day hardware engineering and reports to Srouji. Ternus joins Apple's board on September 1. Arthur Levinson, the company's non-executive chairman for the past 15 years, becomes lead independent director on the same day.
Article Summary: John Ternus will succeed Tim Cook to become the new CEO on September 1, 2026. Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years, will now transition to the role of executive chairman for the board. The transition follows a thoughtful, long-term succession planning process.

Article Title: Sam Altman: Quote of the day by Sam Altman: “I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology... Very soon the world is going to have to contend with incredible video models that can
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Sam Altman

Quote of the day by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman



“I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology... Very soon the world is going to have to contend with incredible video models that can deepfake anyone or kind of show anything you want.”



What the quote by Sam Altman highlights



Understanding deepfake technology



Why advanced video models are a concern



Impact on information and trust



Real-world risks associated with deepfakes



Efforts to manage and regulate AI risks



The role of public awareness



How AI continues to evolve



Other famous quotes by Sam Altman



“AI will probably most likely lead to the end of the world, but in the meantime, there’ll be great companies.”

“We are entering the age of intelligence.”

“The development of superhuman machine intelligence is probably the greatest threat to the continued existence of humanity.”

“Technology doesn’t solve problems by itself; people decide how it is used.”

A broader perspective on technology and responsibility



Final takeaway from Sam Altman's quote







People all over the world are talking more and more about the pros and cons of artificial intelligence as it continues to grow quickly. AI is a part of life now. It has things like writing, designing, and data analysis tools, as well as systems that can make videos and pictures. Experts are also worried about how these new technologies might be misused. Sam Altman , the CEO of OpenAI, is one person who has openly talked about the risks of very advanced AI systems. A lot of people are interested in what he said about deepfake technology and video models because they bring up a real and growing problem. It might be harder to tell what's real and what's fake as AI-generated content becomes more lifelike. This is a big deal for everyone, including people, businesses, and governments. Governments must urgently establish robust ethical frameworks to ensure safety while fostering beneficial AI innovation. Proactive collaboration between policymakers and tech pioneers is essential to build resilient safeguards that uphold truth, security, and human-centric technological advancement.This statement brings up a specific worry: the wrong use of AI to make videos. It says that the technology is new and powerful, but it can also be dangerous if you don't take the right safety precautions.Things that could hurt people, like lying, stealing someone's identity, or changing how people feel about something, are what "really bad stuff" means.The quote talks about how advanced AI systems can make videos that look real but might not show what really happened. This makes it harder for people to check facts and increases the chance of misunderstanding or abuse.Deepfake technology uses AI to change or make videos and audio look real. These systems learn how someone looks and talks by looking at a lot of pictures, videos, and voices. Once they have been trained, they can make new content that looks and acts a lot like the person they are copying.A deepfake video can make it look like someone did or said something they didn't really do. In the past, it was easy to find videos like these. But as AI models have gotten better, the quality has become much more realistic.The quote is about a worry that comes from how fast things are changing. It's getting harder for the average person to find fake content as technology gets better.AI can now make whole scenes instead of just edited clips, thanks to advanced video models. These systems can walk and talk like real people, take pictures that look real, and show feelings that look real.You can use these kinds of skills in a lot of different ways, like making movies, teaching, and simulating. You can also use the same tools to make content that is false or misleading.A fake but realistic video of a famous person could go viral on social media and change people's minds before it is proven to be false. People who know a lot about this say it's one of the most dangerous things. The quote's use of the phrase "show anything you want" shows how this could be used for bad things.Deepfake technology makes people less trusting, which is one of the worst things about it. A lot of people these days learn things by watching videos and looking at pictures. It's hard to know what's real when these sources are easy to change.This could change the way people talk to each other, how news is reported, and how evidence is used in court. If people start to doubt the truth of all digital content, they might not trust reliable sources as much.People are worried about more than just false information spreading; they're also worried about how it will change how society checks the truth in general.There are a lot of real-world risks that come with advanced deepfake technology. Identity theft is when someone uses someone's picture or voice without their permission. Misinformation is when false information is shared as true.Some people have used deepfake videos to spread false political messages or to pretend to be someone else in a money scam. These risks could get worse as the technology gets better if the right safety measures aren't taken.The quote shows that people know about these things happening in real life and need to be ready for them.Governments, tech companies, and research groups are all working to fix the problems that deepfakes and advanced AI systems are causing. Some of the work being done includes making rules about how to use these kinds of technologies and making tools that can find fake content.Some platforms are also putting labels or warnings on content made by AI to make it easier for people to understand what they are looking at. Researchers are also looking into ways to watermark content to show that it was made by AI.The goal of these steps is to stop people from using the technology for bad things while still letting people use it for good things.Another important part of keeping AI safe is making sure that the public knows about it. As technology changes, people need to know how it works and how it can be used for bad things.People can be more careful when they look at things online if they know about deepfake technology. One way to stop false information from spreading is to check multiple sources, make sure the information is correct, and be careful about sharing videos that haven't been checked.The quote indirectly shows how important it is to be aware by pointing out how real and powerful these technologies are becoming.Experts say that AI will keep getting better over the next few years. It looks like video generation models will get faster, more detailed, and easier to use.This progress will bring both good and bad things. It can make it easier to talk to people, learn, and be creative, on the other hand. It might also make the need for stronger protections and responsible use even more important.Sam Altman's statement shows how to find a middle ground between being careful and trying new things.People are talking about deepfakes as part of a bigger conversation about how society handles powerful technologies. New things that people make have always had both good and bad effects. The most important thing has always been how they are used and controlled.The rapid growth of AI makes this process more difficult. People who work for the government, businesses, researchers, and users all need to work together.The quote reminds us that new ideas can help us move forward, but we need to plan and use them carefully to avoid problems.Sam Altman's statement brings up a very important point about the digital world we live in today: the growing power of AI-generated content and the risks that come with it. The quote talks about how things can be misused, which makes people more aware of their own safety, the rules, and safety in general.As AI gets better, it's important to know both the good and bad things about it. It is now possible to make realistic digital content, which opens up new possibilities. However, systems are also needed to make sure that the content is correct, reliable, and used properly.
Article Summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman talks about the risks of using AI to make videos. He says it's hard to tell what's real and what's fake as AI-generated content becomes more lifelike. Governments must urgently establish robust ethical frameworks to ensure safety while fostering AI innovation.

Article Title: Tesla owners are suing Elon Musk’s company and the reason is ‘promise’ made in 2017
Article Text:
Elon Musk with Tesla car

Tesla facing global backlash



The ‘Hardware 3’ problem in Teslas







Tesla is reportedly facing a growing wave of international lawsuits from angry Tesla car owners who claim they were sold a “self-driving” dream that has failed to materialise. The legal battle centers on ‘promises’ made as far back as 2017, with drivers across Europe, Australia and the US now demanding their money back, as per a report by Benzinga. The latest legal challenges arrive just two days before Tesla is set to report its first-quarter earnings.According to the report, the newest legal front opened last week in the Netherlands after a Dutch Tesla owner launched a massive campaign to organise European drivers who paid thousands of dollars for automated driving features they still cannot use in their Tesla cars.This joins a growing list of similar legal battles:An 80-year-old retired attorney in California paid $8,000 for “Full Self-Driving” (FSD) in 2017, and he recently won class-action status. He now represents roughly 3,000 owners seeking full refunds. Another lawsuit is reported to be in Australia where a major law firm is pursuing a separate class action, accusing Tesla of selling cars that are physically incapable of ever becoming fully autonomous.At the heart of these lawsuits is a technical bottleneck known as Hardware 3. This is the computer system installed in millions of Tesla vehicles produced over the last several years. The report notes that Wall Street analysts now say this hardware is likely too outdated to run Elon Musk ’s newest and most sophisticated AI software.Musk told investors in early 2025 that the company would eventually upgrade these older systems for “lifetime” buyers but admitted the process would be “painful and difficult.”In 2020-2021, Tesla offered free upgrades to some early buyers but in 2023, Tesla moved to “Generation 4” hardware which resulted in the owners who paid for “lifetime access” years ago find themselves stuck with obsolete equipment once again.
Article Summary: Legal battle centers on ‘promises’ made as far back as 2017, with drivers across Europe, Australia and the US now demanding their money back. Latest legal challenges arrive just two days before Tesla is set to report its first-quarter earnings.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella: 'Success can cause people to unlearn the habits that...'
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Satya Nadella



“Success can cause people to unlearn the habits that made them successful in the first place.”

Understanding the meaning behind the quote by Satya Nadella



Constant learning

Focus on details

Feedback openness

Adaptability

How success can influence behaviour



Relying on past strategies without reassessment

Reducing experimentation or risk-taking

Becoming less responsive to feedback

The role of habits in long-term performance



Review procedures might be carried out less often

Developing new competencies could become a secondary concern

Careful attention to detail could be overlooked

Learning, adaptation, and consistency



Curiosity and the desire to learn more

Frequent review of decisions and actions taken

Being receptive to various viewpoints

Why is it relevant today



Past successes don’t guarantee future successes.

Effort needs to be continuous rather than getting complacent

One needs to remain adaptable despite past achievements

A more holistic approach to growth and discipline



Go back to basics

Strengthen systems that helped maintain consistency

Notice the beginning of any signs of complacency

"Our industry doesn’t respect traditions; it respects innovations alone.”

“Be bold and passionate and always learning.”

“Empathy helps you to innovate better.”

“Learn-it-all beats the know-it-all.”

A key takeaway from the quote by Satya Nadella







In professional environments, success is often treated as a sign that a particular approach is working. Teams and leaders may continue with the same strategies, assuming they will deliver consistent results. However, in the long run, such practices may develop into a gradual change in behaviour as well. The tasks that used to demand discipline and concentration may become neglected.In this context, one can refer to Microsoft’s CEO Satya Nadella ’s phrase that highlights the impact of success on people’s behaviour. It shows that, in addition to generating profits, success can influence how an individual works.Satya Nadella, a person experienced in making changes within Microsoft Corporation, frequently mentions learning and organisational culture. He also views success as something that requires a specific set of skills to be maintained.It becomes interesting to think about what comes first in this case: success or habit?It is important to understand that this statement does not refer to sudden changes but rather to gradual transformations. According to the statement, once one has succeeded, one will no longer care about the actions that made one successful.They might consist of:After achieving success, one might feel comfortable and therefore think little about making decisions or improvements.Success often brings recognition and stability. While these are positive outcomes, they can also change how individuals evaluate their own methods. Some possible effects include:This does not happen immediately. It develops gradually as confidence in existing methods grows.Habits play an important role in the functioning of both individuals and organisations. They usually emerge from repeated actions and experiences. Once goals have been met successfully, there is little need to preserve such habits. For instance:This observation by Nadella highlights the potential consequences for sustained effectiveness.Among the significant concepts in the statement is the idea of sustaining the behaviours that led to past success.They include:The contemporary workspace offers ample opportunities to achieve success quickly, especially in fields characterised by continuous technological advancement. But sustaining this level of success requires continuous effort.In this scenario:It highlights the need to find a balance point.Achieving success or hitting milestones may mean growth, but it also requires sustaining the discipline to reach them.One might have to:The quotation indicates that success shouldn’t replace continuous effort.Some other notable quotes by Satya NadellaSuccess can sometimes lead to a loss of focus on disciplines that were initially relevant to success. Satya Nadella’s quotation indicates this.One should continue following those disciplines regardless of their achievements.In simple terms, success is not just about reaching a point. It is also about maintaining the behaviours that helped reach there.
Article Summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says success can cause people to unlearn the habits that made them successful in the first place. Success can sometimes lead to a loss of focus on disciplines that were initially relevant to success. The quote indicates that success shouldn’t replace continuous effort.

Article Title: Jensen Huang may claim Nvidia chips can do "a whole bunch of applications" vs Google TPUs, but as Google AI Demis Hassabis says: A lot of people would like to run ...
Article Text:
Training vs. inference is the new battleground

Jensen Huang’s take vs. Demis Hassabis ’ argument

Poll Which company do you believe has the edge in AI chip technology? Google Nvidia Both are equal Neither, there are better options





The rivalry between tech titans Google and Nvidia is said to be heating up as the focus of the artificial intelligence (AI) boom shifts from teaching AI to actually using it to get answers quicker than ever. While Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has claimed his chips (GPUs) are more versatile than Google’s, Google is preparing a major counter-move. At the Google Cloud Next conference this week, the search giant is expected to double down on its custom-made AI chips, known as Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), to meet a massive surge in demand, as per Bloomberg.In the world of AI, there are two main phases: Training (teaching a model like ChatGPT to learn) and Inference (where the AI actually answering your questions). While Nvidia’s chips are currently the “gold standard” for training, Google believes the future lies in specialised chips built specifically for inference that will allow the models to answer queries quickly.“It now becomes sensible to specialize chips more for training or more for inference workloads,” said Google Chief Scientist Jeff Dean, Bloomberg reported.This matters because more people are using AI daily, which means companies need cheaper and more efficient ways to run these models at scale.That’s why, companies like Google itself is using a mix of its TPUs and Nvidia GPUs.Nvidia’s Jensen Huang recently argued that his GPUs are superior because they can handle "a whole bunch of applications" that specialised TPUs simply cannot do. However, Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis sees it differently. He noted that the world’s leading AI labs are increasingly desperate to get their hands on Google’s hardware.“A lot of people would like to run on both,” Hassabis said, highlighting that interest in TPUs has reached an all-time high.Google has a decade-long head start in designing its own chips. Analysts say this gives Google a “home-field advantage” as AI agents (programs that can perform complex tasks on a user's behalf) become the next big thing.“The battleground is shifting towards inference,” said Chirag Dekate, an analyst at Gartner. He noted that Google’s Gemini model is already among the fastest at complex reasoning, largely thanks to the infrastructure Google has built under the hood.Nvidia has already spent a reported $20 billion to bolster its own inference technology by acquiring Groq, that offers inference technology.Moreover, Google has previously touted inference capabilities for its chips. It also considered releasing separate chips for training and inference early on, according to Partha Ranganathan, a vice-president and engineering fellow at Google.
Article Summary: Google is expected to double down on its custom-made AI chips, known as Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), to meet a massive surge in demand. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has claimed his chips are more versatile than Google’s, but Google is preparing a counter-move.

Article Title: Australia's banking regulator on Anthropic's Mythos: We expect financial services companies to ...
Article Text:
APRA : Ensuring system resilience



Fears of Anthropic’s new AI model Mythos reach Canada







Australia’s financial regulators have joined global counterparts in sounding the alarm over Anthropic’s frontier AI model Mythos, which experts say could very well powerful enough to destabilise banking systems by exposing cybersecurity vulnerabilities at scale. According to a report by Reuters, the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) said that it is closely monitoring Mythos along with other regulators. A spokesperson noted: “ASIC engages closely with other regulators, government agencies and the financial sector to understand and respond to changing technologies.” The agency emphasised that that financial services licenses are expected to be proactive in safeguarding customers and clients against risks posed by advanced AI systems.The report by Reuters further adds that the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) echoed ASIC’s concerns, stating it will continue to assess the implications of technological advancements like Mythos to ensure the ongoing safety and resilience of the financial system.The warning from Australia is followed by similar moves in South Korea, where the Financial Supervisory Service (FSS) convened meetings with banks and insurers to review Mythos-related risks.Yonhap news agency reported that the Financial Services Commission (FSC) also held an emergency session with chief information security officers last week.Recently, Executives from Canada’s largest banks and top regulators gathered to discuss the cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic’s new Claude Mythos AI model, amid the growing concerns that the technology could be weaponised to exploit software vulnerabilities. According to a report by The Globe and Mail, the meeting was held by the Canadian Financial Sector Resiliency Group (CFRG), chaired by Bank of Canada COO Alexis Corbett, and included representatives from the Department of Finance, the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), and executives from Canada’s six biggest banks plus Desjardins Group.As per the report, Bank of Canada spokesperson Paul Badertscher emphasised that the meeting was not an emergency one but rather a ‘situational awareness’ session. “It can still hold meetings at the request of its members. ‘Hey guys, we need to pay attention, there is something going on. Let’s get together and talk about this.’ That’s what this was,” he said.
Article Summary: Australia's financial regulators have joined global counterparts in sounding the alarm over Anthropic’s frontier AI model Mythos. Experts say the technology could very well powerful enough to destabilise banking systems by exposing cybersecurity vulnerabilities at scale. Executives from Canada's largest banks and top regulators gathered to discuss the cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's new Claude Mythos AI model.

Article Title: Marc Benioff shares 'stories' of Salesforce AI Agentforce 'helping' companies 'resolve issues' without any human interaction to prove that those saying software is dead are wrong
Article Text:
Salesforce chief executive Marc Benioff (Photo-AP)

The software bears are dead wrong.

At @Pearson, #Agentforce autonomously handles order statuses, refunds & lost access codes — increasing the percentage of customer questions resolved without any human interaction by 40%, says VP Gabriele Bauman.

At @PenFed, our

Agentforce

agent handles password resets & account unlocks for employees — reducing total IT tickets by 40%, says EVP & CIO Shree Reddy. This is the Agentic Enterprise delivering real ROI today — not hype. Agenticware, not Software.

Benioff on why ‘SaaSpocalypse’ won’t happen







Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has a message for everyone who thinks his company is in trouble: you are looking at this all wrong. The comment comes after the software giants watched their stocks fall following ‘fears’ that artificial intelligence (AI) will make traditional software companies obsolete. If AI agents can do the work that companies currently hire armies of employees to do, why would those companies keep paying for expensive software sold on a per-employee basis. Benioff is answering it directly, and to make his case, he has turned to real-world customer stories.According to a report by The Wall Street Journal, and a social media post on X (formerly Twitter), its customers have seen 40% fewer human interactions and 40% fewer IT tickets. Here’s his full post X:Benioff explains that its agents now autonomously handle queries about order statuses, refunds and lost access codes for its customers, and another agent is helping a company employees reset passwords and unlock accounts.Benioff’s argument rests on multiple pillars. First, the leading AI labs cannot simply replace what Salesforce offers, which includes decades of experience in data security, regulatory compliance and industry-specific workflows. He says these cannot be replicated overnight by a general-purpose AI model.The second argument is that customers who might theoretically try to build their own AI-powered alternatives would run serious risks around brand safety and compliance that make Salesforce's established platform far more attractive.Finally, Benioff says that the AI labs themselves see Salesforce as a partner, not a rival. Both Anthropic and OpenAI use Salesforce's workplace app Slack, and Salesforce has integrated their models into Agentforce.Salesforce launched Agentforce in late 2024, allowing businesses to build custom autonomous agents for use in their own workflows. Early reviews were mixed. Salesforce responded by building a new data layer into its technology stack.
Article Summary: Salesforce launched Agentforce in late 2024, allowing businesses to build custom autonomous agents for use in their own workflows. Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has a message for everyone who thinks his company is in trouble: you are looking at this all wrong.

Article Title: Brandi Glanville seeks help from Mark Cuban's Cost Plus Drug Company, leading to drug prices dropping from $30 to $6 amid major medical struggles
Article Text:
PC: Page Six

Brandi Glanville's facial disfigurement and Mark Cuban’s role in her recovery journey



Mark Cuban’s cost model and its impact on patient affordability



Brandi Glanville's medical costs and medication pricing issues reveal major gaps







The recent health condition of Brandi Glanville has highlighted the monetary pressures resulting from complicated health problems. The American television personality and ex-model has suffered from symptoms that have resulted in facial deformity. Although her health issues are alarming, she has also had to consider the financial repercussions, which have become part and parcel of the situation. According to reports by Page Six, although she has insurance coverage, her medication bills have skyrocketed. As a result, she has had to try out other methods to deal with the rising cost of her medications. Among the options that she pursued is getting her medicines from a pharmacy supported by billionaire entrepreneur Mark Cuban The condition that Glanville suffers from was not something that just suddenly appeared; the symptoms took some time to develop to the extent that the condition became difficult to diagnose. The woman allegedly suffered from a condition like brain fog, joint pain, and facial swelling, leading her to seek help from different experts. According to the news reports, she consulted more than 20 doctors in her attempt to get a diagnosis for the cause of the condition.The issue ended up being traced to complications arising from her breast implants. According to the report, one of the implants apparently burst, causing leakage of the silicone to parts around the body, such as the lymph nodes. This reaction caused the body to suffer from many complications. She finally decided to remove the implants from her body and, after doing so, things got better for her.Glanville began working with Strauss and introduced to Mark Cuban's Cost-Plus Drug Company. The company operates on a pricing model that aims to be transparent by showing the base cost of medications along with a fixed markup and a standard fee.The disparity in terms of pricing seems to be rather large. As Strauss noted, some of the drugs that Glanville had been buying on a relatively expensive basis could have been bought much more cheaply using this platform. Some of these drugs, which were being sold for about $30 somewhere else, would apparently go for only $6 or $8 with Cost Plus Drugs.As Mark Cuban mentioned in an interview, his firm exists specifically to eliminate issues such as those discussed above through the simplification of the process. Although this may not solve all the existing problems, it offers another opportunity to patients that might benefit from it, especially those facing limited access to affordable healthcare solutions today.After having the implants removed, there has been an improvement in the condition of Glanville's symptoms. This represents a positive change; however, the problem still has financial repercussions. Depending on further treatment, medications will likely have to be purchased in the future, although their price could decrease, easing some of the ongoing financial burden gradually.In addition to the health issues, the cost of treatment was another important concern. As PageSix mentioned, the approximate amount she paid for her diagnosis and treatment was about $200,000. Despite having insurance, Glanville had to pay for specialists, examinations, and some drugs out-of-pocket, as they were not covered under her health plan.There seemed to be differences in drug prices, which appear to have changed from month to month. For instance, as stated by Rachel Strauss, a consultant in healthcare, the pricing structure does not seem to be consistent for the same medications from one month to another.
Article Summary: Brandi Glanville's medical costs and medication pricing issues reveal major gaps. The American television personality and ex-model has suffered from symptoms that have resulted in facial deformity. Although she has insurance coverage, her medication bills have skyrocketed. Among the options that she pursued is getting her medicines from a pharmacy supported by billionaire entrepreneur Mark Cuban.
